title: Treatment of Advanced Non-small-Cell Lung Cancer with Chinese Herbal Medicine and Chemotherapy
author: Gong, Xu, et al.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: A prospective multi-centered randomized controlled study was conducted, and 266 advanced NSCLC patients were enrolled. 126 patients in control group received Vinorelbine plus cisplatin(NP) chemotherapy combined with symptom-oriented Chinese herbs medication(without qi-nourishing essence-replenishing herbs);140 patients in experimental group received NP chemotherapy combined with qi-nourishing essence-replenishing Chinese herbal medication(Kangliu Zengxiao Decoction and modified Feiyanning Decoction, during and after chemotherapy respectively).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: definitive diagnosis of NSCLC based on imaging, pathological, or cytological evidence;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: diagnosis of inoperable stage IIIb-IV or postoperative recurrent metastatic NSCLC based on the Union for International Cancer Control (UICC) TNM Classification for Malignant Tumors (6th edition) [16];

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status (PS) score ≤ 2 with expectation to complete 2–4 cycles of chemotherapy and survive more than 4 months;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: aged between 18 and 75 years;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: matching the traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) syndrome differentiation of essence and qi deficiency based on the TCM syndrome differentiation of primary bronchial lung cancer in the Guidelines of clinical research on Chinese new herbal medicine [17]:

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: unfulfillment of inclusion criteria;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: severe complications of heart, liver, kidney and/or blood diseases;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: severe infections;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: failure to complete the 2 chemotherapeutic courses;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: PS score>2;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: not matching the TCM syndrome differentiation of essence and qi deficiency.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: violating the protocol criteria;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: not matching the inclusion criteria but enrolled.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: receiving any targeted therapy or any other chemotherapeutic regimen due to disease progression within 3 months after study initiation;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: exhibiting poor compliance that affected efficacy and safety assessments during the study process;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: unsuitable to continue the study due to severe adverse events, complications or special physiological changes;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: less than 3 months of Feiyanning Decoction administration after chemotherapy, or withdrawing from the study at patient’s own discretion;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: administering combined medications that potentially affect efficacy and safety assessments of the study medication;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: withdrawing, lost to follow-up, or dying due to disease-irrelevant reasons before study completion.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: patients suffered from any severe adverse events;

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: any major deficiencies in the clinical protocol or significant deviation was determined to pose difficulties for evaluation.

steps.title: Source and Preparation of Medications
steps.instruction: Kangliu Zengxiao Decoction(KLZX decoction) is composed of Astragalus mongholicus, Ganoderma lucidum, Coptis chinensis, Atractylodes chinensis, etc. Feiyanning Decoction(FYN decoction) contains Astragali Radix, Ganoderma lucidum, Polygonatum sibiricum, Herba Epimedii, Paris polyphylla Smith, beehive, and dried toad skin. All herbs were provided by Longhua Hospital pharmacy, dissolved in 1000 ml water, soaked for 30 min, decocted to 200 ml with gentle heat; then these herbs were dissolved in 500 ml water and decocted to 100 ml. Two decoctions(300 ml) were mixed, and aliquoted into 2 plastic bags (150 ml each), ready for use. The crude drug content was 1.0 g/ml.

steps.title: Source and Preparation of Medications
steps.instruction: 2) Chemotherapeutic agents.

steps.title: Source and Preparation of Medications
steps.instruction: Vinorelbine (NVB) was provided as 10 mg/bottle (Pierre Fabre, France). Cisplatin (DDP) was obtained as 20 mg/bottle (Qilu Pharmaceutical, China).

steps.title: Statistical Analysis
steps.instruction: The SPSS16.0 software was used for statistical analysis. Quantitative data were expressed as mean ± SD, and compared by t test; qualitative data were expressed as rate, and analyzed by the χ2 or Mann-Whitney test. Kaplan-Meier curves were generated to assess survival, with the Log-rank test adopted for comparisons.

steps.title: Survival Analysis
steps.instruction: The median survival time (MST) was 14.87 (95% CI 11.729–18.011) and 12.97 (95% CI 11.252–14.688) months in the experiment and control groups, respectively. (P = 0.027) (Table. 6 and Fig. 1);1-, 3-, 5-, 7-, and 9-year survival rates were 57%,17%,10%,6%,6% in the experiment. However, 53%,8%,2%,0%,0% in control group.1-year, 3-year, 5-year, 7-year, and 9-year survival rates were improved in experiment group(Table. 7).
